ucs.title: [LLM] IndigoZebra (BoxCaon) — Dropbox API abused as C2 channel by non-browser process
ucs.description: Hunts for HTTPS callbacks to Dropbox API endpoints (api.dropboxapi.com / content.dropboxapi.com / notify.dropboxapi.com) from any process other than the legitimate Dropbox client or a browser. This is the load-bearing IOC family for IndigoZebra's BoxCaon backdoor, used in the 2021 campaign against the Afghan presidential office and recurring against Central Asian foreign-affairs targets — the actor specifically chose Dropbox so that C2 traffic blends with normal cloud storage egress.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.url) as urls values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest IN ("api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api-content.dropbox.com","notify.dropboxapi.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com") AND NOT All_Traffic.app IN ("Dropbox.exe","DropboxUpdate.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","explorer.exe","opera.exe") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | join type=left src app [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest Processes.process_name | rename Processes.dest as src Processes.process_name as app | rename Processes.* as *] | search process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR parent IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - count
ucs.kql:
// IndigoZebra / BoxCaon — Dropbox API C2 from non-Dropbox, non-browser process
let _legit_dropbox = dynamic(["dropbox.exe","dropboxupdate.exe","dropboxupdater.exe"]);
let _browsers     = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","arc.exe"]);
let _dropbox_c2   = dynamic(["api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api-content.dropbox.com","notify.dropboxapi.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_dropbox_c2)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_dropbox)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_browsers)
// BoxCaon drops to user-writable paths and runs from there
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\")
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            SampleUrl = any(RemoteUrl),
            SampleCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256, RemoteIP
| where ConnCount >= 3 or DistinctMinutes >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// IndigoZebra / BoxCaon — Dropbox API C2 (Sentinel via ASIM ImNetworkSession)
let _legit_dropbox = dynamic(["dropbox.exe","dropboxupdate.exe","dropboxupdater.exe"]);
let _browsers     = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","arc.exe"]);
let _dropbox_c2   = dynamic(["api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","api-content.dropbox.com","notify.dropboxapi.com","dl.dropboxusercontent.com"]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where (Url has_any (_dropbox_c2)) or (DstHostname has_any (_dropbox_c2))
| extend ProcLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ProcLeaf !in (_legit_dropbox)
| where ProcLeaf !in (_browsers)
| where ActingProcessName has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\")
   or ProcLeaf in ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            SampleUrl = any(Url),
            SampleCmd = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by DvcHostname, ProcLeaf, ActingProcessName, DstIpAddr
| where ConnCount >= 3 or DistinctMinutes >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: IndigoZebra BoxCaon — Dropbox API C2 from Non-Dropbox Process
id: 8c2a5e6b-9b1f-4d3a-9e6b-1a8d3b7e2c41
status: experimental
description: Detects HTTPS callbacks to Dropbox API endpoints from a process that is neither the legitimate Dropbox client nor a browser. IndigoZebra's BoxCaon backdoor (Check Point, July 2021) uses Dropbox as its sole C2 channel.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2021/indigozebra-apt-continues-attack-on-central-asia-with-evolving-tools/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0136/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dns:
    DestinationHostname|contains:
      - 'api.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'content.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api-content.dropbox.com'
      - 'notify.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'dl.dropboxusercontent.com'
  filter_legit_dropbox:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Dropbox.exe'
      - '\DropboxUpdate.exe'
      - '\DropboxUpdater.exe'
  filter_browsers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
      - '\opera.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
  condition: selection_dns and not 1 of filter_*
fields:
  - Image
  - DestinationHostname
  - DestinationIp
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Side-loaded Dropbox clients installed outside Program Files
  - Bespoke backup or DevOps scripts (curl/python) pushing to Dropbox — verify intent
level: high
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.g0136
ucs.rationale: Dropbox-API-as-C2 from a non-Dropbox-client process is a near-bespoke IndigoZebra signature. The xCaon malware family pre-dates BoxCaon (HTTP C2), but the Dropbox-only variant (BoxCaon) was IndigoZebra's signature evolution to bypass network defences in Central Asian gov't networks. Most APTs that abuse cloud storage prefer OneDrive, Google Drive, or GitHub — Dropbox is comparatively rare, which k

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] IndigoZebra spearphish chain — compromised Central Asian gov mailbox → password-protected archive → child binary executed from %TEMP%
ucs.description: Correlates an inbound mail from a Central Asian government domain (or with diplomatic-themed subject) carrying a RAR/7z/ISO/IMG attachment with a subsequent process execution under the same user, where a binary is launched from a user-writable path within 24 hours. IndigoZebra's hallmark initial-access tradecraft is hijacking real government email accounts (Afghan, Uzbek, Kyrgyz MFAs) to send password-protected archives masquerading as presidential / cabinet correspondence — defeating reputation-based mail filtering and most attachment scanners.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as emailTime values(All_Email.subject) as subject values(All_Email.sender) as sender values(All_Email.file_name) as attach_name from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.direction=inbound (All_Email.sender_domain="*.gov.af" OR All_Email.sender_domain="*.gov.kz" OR All_Email.sender_domain="*.gov.kg" OR All_Email.sender_domain="*.gov.uz" OR All_Email.sender_domain="*.gov.tj" OR All_Email.sender_domain="*.gov.tm" OR All_Email.sender_domain="*mfa*" OR All_Email.sender_domain="*mofa*" OR All_Email.subject="*presidential*" OR All_Email.subject="*cabinet*" OR All_Email.subject="*ministry*" OR All_Email.subject="*embassy*") (All_Email.file_name="*.rar" OR All_Email.file_name="*.7z" OR All_Email.file_name="*.iso" OR All_Email.file_name="*.img" OR All_Email.file_name="*.zip") by All_Email.recipient All_Email.message_id | rename All_Email.* as * | rename recipient as user | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as procTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe")) (Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*") (Processes.process_name="*.exe" OR Processes.process_name="*.scr" OR Processes.process_name="*.dll" OR Processes.process_name="*.lnk") by Processes.user Processes.dest Processes.process_name | rename Processes.* as * | rename user as user] | where procTime >= emailTime AND procTime <= emailTime + 86400 | eval delay_min=round((procTime-emailTime)/60,0) | table emailTime procTime delay_min user dest sender subject attach_name parent process_name proc_path cmdline | sort - emailTime
ucs.kql:
// IndigoZebra spearphish — gov-domain sender + archive + execution from user-writable path within 24h
let _gov_domains   = dynamic([".gov.af",".gov.kz",".gov.kg",".gov.uz",".gov.tj",".gov.tm","mfa.","mofa."]);
let _diplo_subjects = dynamic(["presidential","cabinet","ministry","embassy","ambassador","diplomatic","council","foreign affairs"]);
let _archive_ext   = dynamic([".rar",".7z",".iso",".img",".zip"]);
let _droppers      = dynamic([".exe",".scr",".dll",".lnk",".cpl",".hta"]);
let SuspectMail =
    EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
    | where DeliveryAction in ("Delivered","DeliveredAsSpam")
    | where SenderFromDomain has_any (_gov_domains)
         or Subject has_any (_diplo_subjects)
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailAttachmentInfo
        | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
        | where FileName has_any (_archive_ext)
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | project NetworkMessageId, EmailTime = Timestamp,
              SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain, Subject,
              RecipientEmailAddress, AttachmentName = FileName, AttachmentSHA256 = SHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe","olk.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\")
| where FileName has_any (_droppers) or FolderPath endswith ".lnk"
| join kind=inner SuspectMail on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where Timestamp between (EmailTime .. EmailTime + 24h)
| project Timestamp, EmailTime,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, EmailTime),
          DeviceName, AccountUpn,
          SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain, Subject, AttachmentName, AttachmentSHA256,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DroppedBinary = FileName,
          DroppedPath   = FolderPath,
          ChildSHA256   = SHA256,
          ChildCmd      = ProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// IndigoZebra spearphish — Sentinel (OfficeActivity for Exchange + SecurityEvent 4688)
let _gov_domains    = dynamic([".gov.af",".gov.kz",".gov.kg",".gov.uz",".gov.tj",".gov.tm","mfa.","mofa."]);
let _diplo_subjects = dynamic(["presidential","cabinet","ministry","embassy","ambassador","diplomatic","council","foreign affairs"]);
let _archive_ext    = dynamic([".rar",".7z",".iso",".img",".zip"]);
let SuspectMail =
    OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("Receive","MailItemsAccessed","Send")
    | extend SenderDomain = tolower(tostring(split(Sender, "@")[1]))
    | where SenderDomain has_any (_gov_domains) or tolower(Subject) has_any (_diplo_subjects)
    | extend AttachLow = tolower(tostring(ItemAttachments))
    | where AttachLow has_any (_archive_ext)
    | extend Recipient = tolower(tostring(MailboxOwnerUPN))
    | project EmailTime = TimeGenerated, Sender, SenderDomain, Subject, Recipient, AttachLow, MessageId;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\winrar.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\7zfm.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\7zg.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\explorer.exe"
   or ParentProcessName endswith "\\outlook.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\")
| where NewProcessName endswith ".exe" or NewProcessName endswith ".scr" or NewProcessName endswith ".dll"
| extend UserUpn = tolower(strcat(SubjectUserName, "@", SubjectDomainName))
| join kind=inner SuspectMail on $left.UserUpn == $right.Recipient
| where TimeGenerated between (EmailTime .. EmailTime + 24h)
| project TimeGenerated, EmailTime,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, EmailTime),
          Computer, Account, Sender, SenderDomain, Subject,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: IndigoZebra is one of the few clusters where 'mail from a real Central Asian government domain' is itself a high-signal pivot — Check Point's 2021 reporting documented the actor pivoting from a compromised Afghan presidential office mailbox to attack the Kyrgyz, Uzbek, and Turkmen presidencies. Generic phishing-with-attachment hunts FP heavily on legitimate gov correspondence; pinning the predicat
